title: aeluropus littoralis subsp. intermedius
intro: Aeluropus littoralis subsp. intermedius, also known as Saltwort or Sea Saltwort, is a subspecies of a coastal succulent plant that thrives in salt-rich environments. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Aeluropus littoralis subsp. intermedius include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and even exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to create teas, tinctures, and infusions, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and optimal usage.
